hle.canonical_pass_notes: Partial pass (LCD 7/7, KEY 1/1 green; MEMORY 8/9 fail, TIMER 1/3 fail, DMA 2/9 fail, INTERRUPT 2/7 fail). Canonical left null — cable_club has not reached full-pass on AGS Aging. Prior auto-promoted canonical (cac70be3...) was cleared 2026-04-08 per verification-standard memory to avoid locking in a broken state as ground truth. See .agents/handoff/20260408-ags-aging-{memory,lcd,timer,dma,interrupt}.md

cable_club.notes: Re-verified 2026-04-08 post-powercut recovery. User confirmed visual state matches the captured frame. Sections: MEMORY FAIL (8/9), LCD PASS (7/7), TIMER FAIL (1/3), DMA FAIL (2/9), COM N/A, KEY PASS (1/1), INTERRUPT FAIL (2/7). AGS does not use mgba debug log protocol — verification is visual only.

mgba.notes: Stuck on AGS 'LOAD CONFIGURATION / CHECK SUM ERROR / DEFAULT IS SAVED' screen. AGS is trying to load saved configuration data and the runner doesn't provide a save file, so AGS shows the checksum error and never advances past the loader to actually run the tests. Bit-identical hash between mgba and skyemu - same shared failure mode. NOT a bug in either emulator strictly speaking; it's a runner-side test setup issue (we should provide AGS with a save file to skip the config loader). Filed as part of task #11 (test setup needs work).

nanoboyadvance.notes: LONE PASSER under HLE. NBA actually executes the AGS test suite to the results page: MEMORY PASS, LCD PASS, TIMER PASS, DMA PASS, COM N/A (requires link cable hardware), KEY INPUT PASS, INTERRUPT PASS. 6 of 7 sections pass on a single emulator instance.
